title: gDRplots
text: gDRplots package stores functions gDR static visualizations dedicated helpers. package allows users visualize different metrics combinations drugs cell lines interest, processing. gDRplots package supports following metrics: Assumptions input data plot functions require input data.table representation data selected assay added information colData created based gDR data model (MultiAssayExperiment). format provided gDRutils::convert_se_assay_to_dt function. details gDR data model user can find gDRcore package. function naming convention function dedicated one type experiment - marked name symbol sa experiment single-agent (e.g. pheatmap_with_anno_sa) combo - combined experiment (e.g. pheatmap_with_anno_combo). function output Basically functions return ggplot object, except return pheatmap object. indicated function names description, e.g. pheatmap_qc, pheatmap_with_anno_sa, pheatmap_with_anno_combo. group functions return panel group basic plots. name functions always contains word panel. E.g. basic ggplot object - one heatmap selected drug, co-drug one cell line - returned heatmap_combo_with_isoref. heatmap_combo_with_isoref_panel returns ggplot object panel heatmaps selected drug, co-drug list cell lines. Used data: single-agent experiment (synthetic data) codilution experiment (synthetic data) combination experiment (synthetic data) single-agent experiment PRISM data  TIP highly recommended use convert_se_assay_to_dt unify_metadata = TRUE merge_additional_variables = TRUE. prevents errors caused non-unique combinations values DrugName CellLineName columns.

title: gDRplots
text: dose-response plots classic visualizations drug response curves consolidated way. overview many curves presented scale gives user comprehensive view dataset. particularly useful large compound screening datasets, drug universal resistance pattern across cell lines can easily identified. compound generates different sigmoid curves, suggesting inhibitory cytotoxic effects, worth exploring .

text: pheatmap_with_anno_sa function dedicated single-agent experiments generate heatmap. Additionally, rows annotated Tissue columns - drug MOA. Users can also use annotations. function returns heatmap list tables data: matrix annotation vectors.  plot_boxplot_metric_sa_by_CLs plot_boxplot_metric_sa_by_drugs functions dedicated single-agent experiments generate boxplots grouped CellLineName DrugName, respectively.   Additionally, user can colored boxplots secondary variable - Tissue drug MOA respectively.   user can colored points opposite primary variable - DrugName CellLineName respectively.   also possibility generate boxplot grouped chosen variable dedicated DrugName CellLineName. Additionally, n-bottom points colored.

title: gDRplots
text: pheatmap_with_anno_combo function dedicated combination experiments generate heatmap. Additionally, rows annotated Tissue columns - drug MOA co-drug MOA. Users can also use annotations. function returns heatmap list tables data: matrix annotation vectors.  plot_boxplot_metric_sa_by_CLs plot_boxplot_metric_sa_by_drugs functions dedicated single-agent experiments generate boxplots grouped CellLineName combination DrugName DrugName_2, respectively. Additionally, boxplots grouped CellLineName user can colored boxplots secondary variable - Tissue.   also possibility generate boxplot grouped chosen variable dedicated DrugName CellLineName. Additionally, n-top points colored.   experiments drug combinations, users can visualize full response matrix using heatmap_combo_metrics function. Users get view smooth value, HSA (highest single-agent) excess, Bliss excess selected normalization type across combinations concentrations, isobolograms overlaid. , CI plot also shown (Combination Index displayed different ratios two drugs). Users can also use heatmap_combo_metrics_panel get panel heatmaps combo metrics, isobolograms overlaid. CI plot also shown (Combination Index displayed different ratios two drugs). Note default, function returns panel, can also return list panel’s items (param as_list = TRUE).    plot_combination_index function allows users explore dose-sparing effects experiments drug combinations. Combination Index displayed different ratios two drugs.  heatmap_combo_with_isoref function allows users explore reference isobolograms (additive Loewe model) compared measured isobolograms background smooth value normalization type. Users can also use heatmap_combo_with_isoref_panel get panel heatmap selected drug name co-drug name list cell line names.

what: Merging of Manifest, Treatment and Raw data using gDRimport
title: gDRplots
text: running gDR pipeline, data imported using gDRimport package. package facilitates loading data Envision, Tecan, technologies. information supported technologies examples import data can found documentation. plot_plate_stack_info plot_plate functions allow visualizing plate design experiment. plot_plate_stack_info function shows plate information single plot, plot_plate function displays information single plate plot.
code: # Load test data using gDRimport td <- gDRimport::get_test_data()  # Load data from manifest, template, and results files l_tbl <- gDRimport::load_data(   manifest_file = gDRimport::manifest_path(td),   df_template_files = gDRimport::template_path(td),   results_file = gDRimport::result_path(td) )  # Merge the manifest, treatments, and raw data merged_data <- gDRcore::merge_data(   l_tbl$manifest,   l_tbl$treatments,   l_tbl$data )  # Visualize the plate design of the experiment plate_stack_vis <- plot_plate_stack_info(merged_data) plate_vis <- plot_plate(merged_data, "ReadoutValue") plate_stack_vis[["201904197a"]] plate_vis[["201904197a"]]

title: gDRplots
text: Generating visualization PRISM DepMap generetde selected drug requires dedicated inputs. First part based PRISM data prapred one functions: - prep_dt_response_metric_sa based “Metrics” assay single-agent experiment - prep_dt_response_dose_sa based “Averaged” assay single-agent experiment - prep_dt_response_scores based “scores” assay combination experiment - prep_dt_response_metric_diff based “Metrics” assay combination experiment second part based DepMap data prapred one functions: prep_dt_depmap_meta metadata columns selected cell line annotations donor demographics prep_dt_depmap_feat selected feature volcano visualization also required object calculated linear associations genarated prep_dt_assoc function.
code: sel_drug <- unique(dt_norm_prism_sa$DrugName)[1] print(sel_drug) #> [1] "GDC-8025" dt_response <- prep_dt_response_metric_sa(   dt_metrics = dt_metrics_prism_sa,   d_name = sel_drug,   normalization_type = "RV",   metric = "x_mean" ) meta_data_path <-    system.file("depmap_data/Model.csv.gz", package = "gDRtestData")  meta_col <- "OncotreeLineage" obj_depmap_meta <- prep_dt_depmap_meta(     meta_data_path = meta_data_path,      metadata_col = meta_col) feat_data_path <- system.file("depmap_data", package = "gDRtestData")  feature_set <- "OmicsCNGene" obj_depmap_feat <- prep_dt_depmap_feat(     feat_data_path = feat_data_path,     meta_data_path = meta_data_path,     feature_set = feature_set)  feature_set_2 <- "OmicsSomaticMutationsMatrixDamaging" obj_depmap_feat_2 <- prep_dt_depmap_feat(     feat_data_path = feat_data_path,     meta_data_path = meta_data_path,     feature_set = feature_set_2,     with_decoding = TRUE) obj_assoc_meta <- prep_dt_assoc(   dt_response = dt_response,   dt_depmap = obj_depmap_meta$dt_depmap,   selected_feat_meta_col = obj_depmap_meta$selected_feat_meta_col)  obj_assoc_feta <- prep_dt_assoc(   dt_response = dt_response,   dt_depmap = obj_depmap_feat$dt_depmap,   selected_feat_meta_col = obj_depmap_feat$selected_feat_meta_col)

text: drug1_name_vec character vector drug names plotted (identifiers DrugName) drug2_name_vec character vector co-drug names plotted (identifiers DrugName_2) dt_metrics data.table representing data Metrics assay, outputted gDRutils::convert_se_assay_to_dt(se, "Metrics") combo SummarizedExperiment dt_scores data.table representing data scores assay, outputted gDRutils::convert_se_assay_to_dt(se, "scores") combo SummarizedExperiment normalization_type_vec character vector normalization types selected one : "GR" ("GRvalue") "RV" ("RelativeViability") metric character vector names metric;  chosen : "xc50" ("GR50" "IC50" - respectively depending normalization_type), "x_max" ("GR Max" "E Max") "x_mean" ("GR Mean" "RV Mean") metric_scores character vector names combo metric; chosen : "hsa_score"("Bliss Excess GR" "Bliss Excess RV" - respectively depending normalization_type), "bliss_score" ("Bliss Score GR" "Bliss Score RV") fit_source string source name metrics meta_data_path string path metadata file describing cancer models/cell lines referenced dataset contained within DepMap portal. usually file named Model.csv Model.csv.gz. feat_data_path string path directory containing molecular feature set file load DepMap. feature_sets character vector containing names molecular feature sets load DepMap. names also correspond file names containing feature data (without extension, assumed csv csv.gz) metadata_columns character vector metadata columns load DepMap cell lines clear_taxonomy_info logical flag whether remove taxonomy information gene names table molecular feature sets DepMap. with_decoding logical whether feature OmicsArmLevelCNA, OmicsSomaticMutationsMatrixHotspot OmicsSomaticMutationsMatrixDamaging encoded 0-1 scheme
